title: Cnemidaria karsteniana
intro: Cnemidaria karsteniana, also known as the "tropical club moss" or "resurrection fern," is a type of epiphytic lycopod plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cnemidaria karsteniana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used in the form of a decoction or tincture.
